name: No.JS
description: Build dynamic, reactive web applications using nothing but HTML attributes. No build step. No virtual DOM. No transpiler. No JSX. Just HTML.

router.base: string — base path for the app (default: '/')
router.templates: string — directory for file-based route templates (default: 'pages')
router.ext: string — file extension for auto-resolved route templates (default: '.tpl')
router.focusBehavior: string — 'none' (default) or 'auto' — moves focus to new content after SPA navigation for accessible navigation

options.retries: number — automatic retries on failed requests (default: 0)
options.retryDelay: number — delay between retries in ms (default: 1000)
options.csrf: object — CSRF token configuration ({ token, header })
options.templates: object — template loading settings ({ cache: true })
options.sanitize: boolean — sanitize HTML in bind-html (default: true)
options.dangerouslyDisableSanitize: boolean — explicit opt-out of HTML sanitization (default: false)
options.exprCacheSize: number — maximum expression cache entries using LRU eviction (default: 500)
options.maxEventListeners: number — maximum event listeners per element (default: 100)
options.devtools: boolean — enable browser devtools panel (default: false)
options.stores: object — define global stores at config time

init.description: Initialize NoJS on a root element
init.notes: Called automatically by CDN script. Only needed for npm/ESM usage. Returns a Promise that resolves when initialization is complete (including plugin init hooks). Fires plugins:ready event.

options.loadPath: string — URL pattern for locale files, e.g. 'locales/{locale}/{ns}.json'

interceptor.description: Register request/response interceptors for fetch directives. Supports async functions. When registered inside a plugin, interceptors are tagged with the plugin name and receive redacted responses (sensitive headers/URLs stripped) unless the plugin is trusted.
interceptor.notes: Request interceptors can return sentinel objects: { [NoJS.CANCEL]: true } to abort, { [NoJS.RESPOND]: data } to short-circuit. Response interceptors can return { [NoJS.REPLACE]: data } to replace response data. Each interceptor has a 5-second timeout.

use.description: Register a plugin with the framework
use.notes: Plugin object: { name, version?, capabilities?, install(NoJS, options), init?(NoJS), dispose?(NoJS) }. Named functions accepted as shorthand. Duplicate names rejected. Options: { trusted: true } grants unredacted header access.

global.description: Register a reactive global variable accessible as $name in all expressions
global.notes: Name must start with a letter, contain only [a-zA-Z0-9_]. Reserved names (store, route, router, etc.) are rejected. Functions, eval, and Function references are rejected. Objects are sanitized (prototype keys stripped).

dispose.description: Tear down the entire NoJS instance
dispose.notes: Disposes plugins in reverse order (3s timeout each), clears interceptors, globals, stores, event bus, and router. Returns a Promise. After dispose, NoJS.init() can be called again.

CANCEL.description: Read-only Symbol sentinel for request interceptors — cancels the request
CANCEL.notes: Throws a DOMException with name 'AbortError'. Handled by the error template if present.

RESPOND.description: Read-only Symbol sentinel for request interceptors — short-circuits with mock data
RESPOND.notes: The value becomes the parsed response data, bypassing the network request.

REPLACE.description: Read-only Symbol sentinel for response interceptors — replaces response data
REPLACE.notes: The value replaces the entire response data.

store.notes: Returns the _stores object. Access individual stores: NoJS.store.auth

notify.description: Notify all store watchers after external JavaScript mutations to NoJS.store

router.notes: Provides push(), replace(), back(), forward(). push() and replace() return Promises.

locale.description: Get or set the current locale

baseApiUrl.description: Get or set the API base URL

version.note: In template expressions, use window.NoJS.version (NoJS is not on the expression evaluator's allow-list)

utilities.evaluate: Evaluate an expression string in a context
utilities.findContext: Find the nearest reactive context for a DOM element
utilities.processTree: Process a DOM subtree to activate directives
utilities.resolve: Resolve a property path from a context
